LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT IP CLEANING S.P.A. MOTION TO DISMISS FOR LACK OF JURISDICTION CONTINUED FROM APR-20-2010 TO LAW AND MOTION AT JUN-11-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. HEARING HELD AND ARGUED. MATTER CONTINUED TO JUNE 11, 2010 TO ALLOW LIMITED DISCOVERY ON JURISDICTION ONLY. FURTHER BRIEFING BY PLAINTIFF DUE 6/7/2010; FURTHER BRIEFING BY DEFENDANT DUE 6/9/2010; COURTESY COPIES TO BE SENT DIRECTLY TO DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KATY ANNE BERSAMIN, CSR #11722 ***[06/11/10 HEARING DATE & FURTHER BRIEFING SCHEDULE VACATED PER EX PARTE ORDER OF 06/03/10 & NEW SCHEDULE SET]***
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-20-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT IP CLEANING S.P.A.'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR LACK OF JURISDICTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT THIS COURT HAS PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT IN EITHER FORM, GENERAL OR SPECIFIC. DEFENDANT DOES NOT HAVE SUBSTANTIAL, SYSTEMATIC, AND CONTINUOUS TIES TO CALIFORNIA TO SUPPORT GENERAL JURISDICTION. DEFENDANT IS A FOREIGN CORPORATION AS ARE ALL THE PARTIES. NO SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT HAS PROPERTY, OFFICES, FACILITIES, EMPLOYEES, OR BANK ACCOUNTS IN CALIFORNIA, OR THAT DEFENDANT MAINTAINS MARKETING EFFORTS IN CALIFORNIA. NO SPECIFIC JURISDICTION: THE CONTRACT WAS NEGOTIATED OUTSIDE OF CALIFORNIA AND PROVIDES ALL DISPUTES ARE TO BE GOVERNED BY ITALIAN LAW AND ANY UNRESOLVED CONTROVERSY SHALL BE EXCLUSIVELY SETTLED BY THE COURT OF MILAN. A MOTION TO COMPEL WILL NOT ALTER THE OUTCOME OF THIS MOTION. DEFENDANT WOULD LIKELY INCUR SIGNIFICANT EXPENSE IN DEFENDING THIS ACTION IN CALIFORNIA. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Jurisdiction;
Off Calendar Jun-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT IP CLEANING S.P.A.'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR LACK OF JURISDICTION is granted. Plaintiff fails to establish that this court has personal jurisdiction over defendant in either form, general or specific. Defendant does not have substantial, systematic, and continuous ties to California to support general jurisdiction. Defendant is a foreign corporation as are all the parties. No showing that defendant has property, offices, facilities, employees, or bank accounts in California, or that defendant maintains marketing efforts in California. No specific jurisdiction: the contract was negotiated outside of California and provides all disputes are to be governed by Italian law and any unresolved controversy shall be exclusively settled by the Court of Milan. A motion to compel will not alter the outcome of this motion. Defendant would likely incur significant expense in defending this action in California. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2010 Continued To Jul-30-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Jurisdiction;

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Jurisdiction;
Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Continued To Jun-11-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT IP CLEANING S.p.a. Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Jurisdiction. Appearances: Ramiz I. Rafeedie, Esq. of K&L Gates, (415) 249-1075 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Richard J. Mooney, Esq. of Holme Roberts & Owen LLP (415) 268-1987 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Hearing held and argued. Ruling: matter continued to June 11, 2010 to allow limited discovery on jurisdiction only. Further briefing by Plaintiff due 6/7/2010; further briefing by defendant due 6/9/2010; courtesy copies to be sent directly to Department 302. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Katy Anne Bersamin, CSR # 11722
